title: Dizygotheca reginae
intro: Dizygotheca reginae, also known as the Queen's Crested Calathea or the Queen's Crown, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dizygotheca reginae include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dizygotheca reginae is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
